title: senecio deformis
intro: Senecio deformis, also known as Ragwort, is a perennial plant species native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its yellow or orange daisy-like flowers and jagged, toothed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs, and treat respiratory issues. The health benefits of Senecio deformis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, although it is essential to note that excessive consumption can be toxic due to the presence of pyrrolizidine alkaloids.
